The public surface: domain types only. Repositories, services, and the wallet DB layer are SDK-internal; during the migration the web imports them from ./temporary instead, so that deleting that entry at the end compiler-enforces the boundary.
exports:comments../gift-card-config: Node-loadable escape hatch: vite.config.ts imports the web's gift-card-config module, and vite evaluates its config under plain Node, where the SDK's extensionless relative imports don't resolve (the main entry has runtime re-exports, so Node's type stripping doesn't save it). This module's graph imports only zod, so Node can load it. Keep it that way — adding a relative import to it breaks the Vercel build.
exports:comments../temporary: Migration-only re-exports of SDK internals (repositories, services, DB types) that the web still imports mid-extraction. Deleted once the extraction completes.
exports:comments../temporary.server: Same as ./temporary but for server-only modules, kept separate so `.server` files never enter the client module graph — React Router forbids client code reaching them.
